artifacts.description: This figure illustrates a typical voltage recovery profile for a short-circuit fault at Supergrid Voltage (400/275 kV) on the Onshore Transmission System cleared in less than 140 milliseconds by two circuit breakers (2-ended circuit). The top portion shows a simple one-line diagram of a 400/275 kV transmission line with circuit breakers at both ends. The bottom plot displays voltage (U/Un, normalized to nominal voltage) versus time, showing the fault ride-through requirement defined in CC.6.3.15.1(a)(i). The voltage profile starts at 100%, drops to 0% at fault inception, remains at 0% until 80ms when the first breaker clears, jumps to approximately 50% voltage, then rises to approximately 80% at 120ms when the second breaker clears, and gradually recovers toward 90% thereafter. A horizontal dashed line at 90% marks the target recovery level. The annotation "Indicative Voltage and Time Durations" with a vertical dashed line at 120ms emphasizes the critical clearing time. This figure provides a concrete example of the fault ride-through capability that generating units, power park modules, and DC converters must withstand when connected to the transmission system, demonstrating the stepwise voltage recovery characteristic of sequential breaker operation in a two-ended fault scenario.
